title: uva baumannii
intro: Uva baumannii, also known as the Bearberry or Kinnikinnick, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to North America, belonging to the heath family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, astringent, and diuretic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of arbutin. The health benefits of Uva baumannii include its ability to treat urinary tract infections, reduce inflammation, and support kidney function. Uva baumannii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried leaves, which can be used to make infusions for its medicinal and health benefits.
